source.title: Tank Explosions at Midland Resource Recovery

event.top_event: Explosion of pressurized vessel during chemical treatment and draining operations
actions_taken: Research into alkyl hypochlorite chemistry conducted
SPSI hired to drain remaining MP odorizers
MRR ceased use of sodium hypochlorite treatment
MRR implemented proprietary process for odor removal
event.summary: Two explosions occurred at Midland Resource Recovery facility in Philippi, West Virginia on May 24, 2017 and June 20, 2017. The May 24 incident killed two workers (including company founder/president) and severely injured another. The June 20 incident fatally injured a contractor from Specialized Professional Services, Inc. Both explosions occurred during draining of Peerless MP odorizers that had been chemically treated with sodium hypochlorite. The probable cause was reactive, unstable chemicals generated during treatment of uncharacterized liquid residues in the vessels. MRR lacked effective process safety management systems to identify and control hazards from reactive chemicals.
recommendations: OSHA should establish or revise standards to require companies dealing with reactive chemistry to conduct formal hazard analyses and implement robust safety management systems
EPA should strengthen Risk Management Plan (RMP) regulations to address reactive chemical hazards
Companies should avoid treating uncharacterized waste materials with sodium hypochlorite due to explosive hazard potential
Companies should conduct thorough evaluation of reactive chemistry under design and abnormal conditions
Implement formal hazard identification processes before decommissioning equipment
Install pressure relief protection on vessels containing reactive chemicals
Establish complete chemical characterization protocols before treatment operations

hazards.name: Reactive chemical hazard from sodium hypochlorite treatment of uncharacterized residues
Hydrogen gas generation from aluminum-sodium hydroxide reaction
Overpressure in sealed vessels lacking relief protection

threats.name: Uncharacterized liquid residues in MP odorizers during chemical treatment
Closed valve trapping air pocket in middle chamber during sodium hypochlorite filling
Aluminum components exposed to sodium hydroxide solution
Rapid hydrogen gas generation in sealed vessel
Methanol or other contaminants present in uncharacterized residues
Methyl hypochlorite formation from methanol and sodium hypochlorite reaction

consequences.name: Release of process water and chemical vapors

controls.name: Chemical characterization and hazard analysis of residues before treatment
controls.barrier_role: Identify reactive chemical hazards before treatment operations
controls.lod_basis: Prevent introduction of incompatible materials into treatment process

supporting_text: MRR technicians do not perform any testing to analyze or characterize what chemicals are present in the MP odorizer
MRR typically must rely solely on verbal communication from the client company of the likely material present in the odorizer equipment
some companies do not have a written record or safety data sheet (SDS) for the full range of possible materials inside the tank

controls.name: Process Safety Management (PSM) system implementation
controls.barrier_role: Establish systematic approach to identify and control reactive chemical hazards
controls.lod_basis: Prevent reactive chemical incidents through formal management system

supporting_text: MRR lacked an effective safety management system to identify and control hazards from reactive chemicals
MRR had no formal hazard identification process in place to analyze or characterize what chemicals were inside the odorizer vessels
Lack of a process safety management system

controls.name: Pressure relief protection on treatment vessels
controls.barrier_role: Prevent overpressure in sealed vessels during chemical reactions
controls.lod_basis: Relieve pressure from unexpected chemical reactions

supporting_text: MRR did not install pressure relief on the tanks because the company thought it was not needed
MRR technicians typically observed an operating pressure of -0.25 to 5 psig during the chemical treatment process. The tanks are rated at 125 to 1,000 psig

controls.name: Valve position control and verification during filling operations
controls.barrier_role: Prevent air pocket formation in sealed chambers during chemical treatment
controls.lod_basis: Ensure proper filling procedure to avoid trapped air pockets

supporting_text: SPSI observed, on the MP odorizer that exploded on May 24, 2017, that the valve between the top and middle chambers was in the open position, counter to what MRR had communicated as the technique used to fill the vessels with sodium hypochlorite
MRR personnel filled the MP odorizer with sodium hypochlorite solution, keeping the valve between the middle and top chambers closed

controls.name: Complete liquid drainage from all vessel chambers before transport
controls.barrier_role: Remove uncharacterized residues that could react during treatment
controls.lod_basis: Eliminate reactive chemical residues before treatment operations

supporting_text: due to the design of the odorizer itself and the decommissioning process MRR established, MRR technicians do not have a way to remove all the uncharacterized liquid from the bottom chamber or the middle chamber
MRR has received tanks at its Philippi site that still contained excessive amounts of liquid

controls.name: Pressure monitoring and alarm system during draining operations
controls.barrier_role: Detect unexpected pressure buildup during draining
controls.lod_basis: Alert operators to abnormal conditions before vessel failure

supporting_text: SPSI had installed an additional (new) valve and pressure gauge into the piping coming off the middle chamber
The pressure gauge indicated zero pressure in the MP odorizer
One SPSI witness explained, '[I] saw the line kick a little bit, which was sort of a surprise…because the gauge had said zero [pressure]'
SPSI's procedure called for halting the operation if the pressure gauge showed more than 30 psig

controls.name: Worker positioning and protective barriers during draining operations
controls.barrier_role: Reduce worker exposure to explosion hazard
controls.lod_basis: Provide physical protection from vessel fragments

supporting_text: Figure 15 above shows three SPSI workers positioned behind one of three concrete barriers set up before the June 20, 2017, explosion
The CSB was not able to determine conclusively the intended purpose of these barriers
A portion of the MP chamber struck and fatally injured the field supervisor

controls.name: Avoidance of sodium hypochlorite treatment of uncharacterized materials
controls.barrier_role: Prevent formation of explosive alkyl hypochlorites
controls.lod_basis: Eliminate conditions for alkyl hypochlorite formation

supporting_text: Companies should avoid treating uncharacterized waste materials with sodium hypochlorite because of the potential explosive hazards associated with its complex reactive chemistry
alkyl hypochlorites have been identified in such waste streams and 'have also been cited as the reason for explosions'
methyl hypochlorites in particular being classified as a high explosive having a similar explosive potential to TNT

controls.name: Regulatory oversight and enforcement of reactive chemical safety standards
controls.barrier_role: Ensure companies comply with safety requirements for reactive chemicals
controls.lod_basis: Regulatory framework to prevent reactive chemical incidents

supporting_text: Persisting gaps in federal safety regulations for reactive chemical hazards
The CSB reiterates recommendations to the U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) and the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
